'(it) extended / encompassed / was vast enough for' — to be wide, spacious, or ample; to take in or embrace something fully within one's capacity. It conveys all-embracing breadth.
A masculine singular noun with the attached possessive pronoun -hu ('his'). The -u ending is nominative, the case typically used for the subject of a sentence (the doer or the thing being described).
'His seat / His throne-footstool' — a kursī is a seat, chair, or footstool, signifying a place of authority, dominion, and majesty. With the pronoun it denotes that seat as belonging to Him.

A definite feminine plural noun with the prefix 'al-' ('the'). The -i ending marks the genitive case, which typically links the word to a preceding noun ('… OF the heavens') or follows a preposition. In the sound feminine plural, this -i form also covers what would be an object role.
The heavens / the skies — the plural of 'heaven', referring to the skies above and the celestial realms; the elevated expanse over the earth.
and the earth / land definite accusative ending (-a)
A feminine noun, prefixed with 'and' and 'the.' The -a ending is accusative, the case typically marking the object of a verb (the thing acted upon) or a noun governed by certain particles.
'and the earth' — the ground, land, or the planet earth; the world we stand upon, often paired with the heavens to mean all of creation.

'wa' is 'and'. 'lā' is a negating particle; placed before a verb of command it expresses prohibition ('do not ...'), and before other verbs/nouns it expresses negation ('not ...'). It carries no case ending.
And not / but not — a connective 'and' joined to a negating particle that rules out or forbids what follows.
he / it (does) weary / burden indicative ending (-u) him

A present-tense verb, third-person masculine singular ('he/it wearies'), with attached object pronoun -hu ('him'). The -u before the suffix is the indicative ending, marking a plain statement of fact ('it wearies / does burden').
ا-و-د (ʾ-w-d) — to weigh down, burden, weary
'it wearies/burdens Him' — to weigh down, tire, or overtax someone, the object being 'Him.' The verb conveys a load that strains or exhausts.
guarding / preserving nominative ending (-u) the two of them / both of them

A masculine verbal noun with the attached pronoun -humā ('the two of them' — a special dual form for exactly two). The -u ending is nominative, the case typically used for the subject of a sentence (here, the act of guarding, presented as the subject).
'the safeguarding of them both / preserving the two of them' — the act of guarding, keeping, or maintaining, here directed at two things together. Ḥifẓ is careful preservation and protection.

A connective particle plus an independent pronoun. The wa- prefix means 'and', joining clauses or words. 'Huwa' is the detached third-person masculine singular pronoun; it carries no case ending, its final -a being fixed.
'And He' — the connective 'and' joined to the standalone pronoun 'He' (a single masculine person or thing), often introducing a new clause about the same subject.
the high / exalted definite nominative ending (-u)

A definite adjective/noun in an intensive pattern ('the Most High'). The -u ending is nominative, the case typically used for the subject of a sentence or for an appositive descriptive title attached to such a subject.
ع-ل-و (ʿ-l-w) — to be high, lofty, exalted
'the Most High / the Exalted' — lofty, elevated, supreme in rank and majesty above all else. As a divine name it affirms transcendence and highest dignity.
the great / immense definite nominative ending (-u)

A definite adjective/noun in an intensive pattern ('the Magnificent'). The -u ending is nominative, the case typically used for the subject of a sentence or for an appositive descriptive title attached to such a subject.
ع-ظ-م (ʿ-ẓ-m) — to be great, mighty, immense
'the Most Great / the Immense' — vast, mighty, and tremendous in greatness and grandeur. As a divine name it conveys overwhelming magnificence and majesty.
